message:
chore(beads): close 8 beads (2 epics, 5 tasks, 1 pre-existing)

Closed:
- bd-0loi (epic) + bd-0loi.1, bd-0loi.2: version strings dynamic
- bd-hpt8 (epic) + bd-hpt8.1, bd-hpt8.2, bd-hpt8.3: robustness
- bd-hzhd.1: relative test dates helper
- bd-oxr3: graph/insights sync (pre-existing, already done)

Remaining open: 4 P3 features (weights, limits, baselines, test isolation)
+ 4 blocked P3-P4 features (alerts, drift, search, background threshold)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
-{"id":"bd-0loi","title":"Epic: Make all version strings dynamic and self-consistent","description":"Currently, 5+ locations in the codebase hard-coded version strings like 1.0.0, 0.1.0, or 1.0 instead of deriving from CARGO_PKG_VERSION. This session fixed the known instances (commit 12a6401), but the underlying pattern needs a systematic guard to prevent regression. When the package version in Cargo.toml changes (e.g., 0.1.0 to 0.2.0), hard-coded version strings silently become wrong. For a triage engine where determinism and trust are core values, emitting incorrect version metadata undermines the tool credibility. This epic covers adding CI-level guards and documentation to prevent future regressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.054022207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:57.054022207Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robustness","versioning"]}
-{"id":"bd-0loi.1","title":"Add grep-based CI check that catches hard-coded version strings","description":"Add a CI step (or a cargo test) that greps the src/ directory for literal version strings that should instead use env!(CARGO_PKG_VERSION). The check should flag patterns like '"1.0.0"' or '"0.1.0"' in non-test Rust code that appear in version-like contexts (struct fields named 'version', 'schema_version', etc.).

IMPLEMENTATION APPROACH:
- Preferred: a #[test] function in lib.rs or a dedicated test file that uses include_str! or reads src/ files and asserts no hard-coded version patterns exist outside of test code
- Alternative: a shell script in scripts/ invoked by CI that greps for suspicious patterns
- The test should allowlist known safe uses (e.g., the BLURB_VERSION HTML markers in agents.rs which are guarded by the agent_blurb_version_matches_constant test)

WHY THIS MATTERS:
Five instances of hard-coded versions were found and fixed in commit 12a6401. Without a guard, the same mistake will recur the next time someone adds a new struct with a version field. The robot envelope, pages meta, triage meta, and schema structs all need version fields — its easy to forget env!() when writing new code.

FILES TO CHECK:
- src/analysis/triage.rs (TriageMeta.version)
- src/robot.rs (RobotSchemas.schema_version, RobotEnvelope.version)
- src/export_pages.rs (PagesMeta.version)
- src/analysis/recipe.rs (default_version())
- src/analysis/brief.rs (BriefMeta.version)
- Any new file that adds a version/schema_version field","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:13.274235658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:13.274235658Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","robustness","versioning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0loi.1","depends_on_id":"bd-0loi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:13.274235658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-0loi.2","title":"Extract BLURB_VERSION into the agent blurb marker string dynamically","description":"In agents.rs, BLURB_VERSION (line 15) is a u32 constant set to 1, but the AGENT_BLURB raw string literal (line 25) hard-codes the marker as '<!-- bv-agent-instructions-v1 -->'. When BLURB_VERSION is bumped to 2, someone must manually update the marker inside the raw string — which is easy to forget.

CURRENT STATE:
- A guard test (agent_blurb_version_matches_constant) was added in commit 12a6401 that will catch the mismatch at test time
- But the ideal fix is to construct the blurb dynamically so the version cant drift

CHALLENGE:
AGENT_BLURB is a const &str (raw string literal), which cant contain format!() or concat!(). Options:
1. Use lazy_static! or std::sync::LazyLock to construct the blurb at runtime with the version interpolated
2. Use a const fn that concatenates using const_format crate
3. Use a build.rs step that generates the blurb with the correct version
4. Keep the current approach (const raw string + guard test) and accept the manual sync — the test makes it safe

RECOMMENDATION:
Option 4 (keep guard test) is simplest and already implemented. Only upgrade to option 1/2/3 if the version needs to change frequently. Document the coupling in a code comment near BLURB_VERSION.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.086663623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:46.793366881Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robustness","versioning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0loi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-0loi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:28.086663623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-0loi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-0loi.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:46.792336461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

This session fixed the known instances (commit 12a6401), but the underlying pattern needs a systematic guard to prevent regression. When the package version in Cargo.toml changes (e.g., 0.1.0 to 0.2.0), hard-coded version strings silently become wrong. For a triage engine where determinism and trust are core values, emitting incorrect version metadata undermines the tool credibility.  {"id":"bd-0yfi","title":"Improve analysis/diff.rs unit test coverage","description":"analysis/diff.rs has 935 lines but only 9 tests (0.9/100 lines). Add tests for uncovered diff computation functions, edge cases, and snapshot comparison logic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T03:09:20.081528353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:13:14.026173875Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T03:13:14.022955045Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-14s","title":"Port --robot-forecast sprint filtering parity","description":"Add legacy-equivalent sprint-target filtering for --robot-forecast and tighten ETA-model fields/summary parity with conformance fixture coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-18T02:43:18.557143985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T02:54:51.604586203Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T02:54:51.604569001Z","close_reason":"Implemented --forecast-sprint filtering parity and legacy-compatible all-vs-single forecast filtering semantics with conformance tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["forecast","parity","porting","robot"]}
 {"id":"bd-155","title":"Fix test isolation: 18 unit tests fail under default parallelism","description":"18 unit tests in agents, loader, correlation, export_pages, and brief modules fail when run with default thread count but pass with --test-threads=1. Tests likely share temp directories or env vars. CI uses --test-threads=4 which may mask the issue.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:20:00.403617901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:58:00.078351547Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T06:58:00.078331960Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-155","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Tests pass now; transient rch worker issue","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:44:11Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-155","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Root cause: rch worker /tmp on btrfs has a space constraint (ENOSPC errno 28). All 18 failing tests use tempfile::tempdir() which defaults to /tmp. Fix: set TMPDIR=/data/tmp when running via rch. All 597 tests pass with this workaround. Permanent fix: update CI config or rch hook to set TMPDIR.","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:57:29Z"},{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-155","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CI (GitHub Actions) unaffected. Only rch workers have /tmp btrfs constraint. Workaround: TMPDIR=/data/tmp rch exec -- cargo test. Not a code change needed.","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:57:59Z"}]}
@@ -257,12 +257,12 @@
 {"id":"bd-fdmb","title":"Preview server should reject symlink escapes and avoid recursive symlink loops","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:36:03.415178962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:36:15.725887589Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T15:36:15.724276590Z","close_reason":"Preview request resolution now enforces bundle-root confinement, recursive preview scans track visited canonical directories to avoid symlink loops, and the live-reload token uses millisecond precision; added unix regressions and verified with cargo check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-frl1","title":"Fix wizard repair routing for missing output path","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:53:28.069300609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T17:59:31.553196396Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T17:59:31.548041164Z","close_reason":"Fixed saved-config repair routing so missing output path returns to target settings; added regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gah","title":"Add search functionality to graph and insights TUI modes","description":"Board and history modes have / search with n/N cycling. Graph and insights modes lack search. Add / keybinding for graph mode to filter nodes by ID/title match, and for insights mode to filter ranked items.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-27T19:03:04.838974667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:21:21.967159501Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:21:21.967139904Z","close_reason":"Added / search with n/N match cycling to both graph and insights TUI modes. Search matches by issue ID or title. Includes active search input handling, match counter display, and Escape to cancel. 4 new unit tests added. All tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-hpt8","title":"Epic: Robustness improvements from deep code review","description":"During a comprehensive deep code review of all 35 source files, several robustness improvements were identified. Most bugs found were already fixed in prior commits, but some hardening opportunities remain.

## Review Coverage
All 35 .rs files under src/ were reviewed across multiple passes by specialized agents, checking for:
- Panics from unwrap/expect in production code
- Division by zero without guards
- Integer overflow/truncation (as u16, as u32)
- Off-by-one errors in loops and indexing
- Logic errors in conditionals
- Resource leaks
- SQL injection (export_sqlite.rs)

## Result
Only 1 bare .unwrap() exists in production code (analysis/advanced.rs:701) and it is safe by algorithmic invariant. All other unwraps are either in test code or use safe variants (unwrap_or, unwrap_or_default, unwrap_or_else).

## Previously Fixed Bugs (for context)
- plan.rs track ID numbering gaps (commit 1c005f4)
- brief.rs markdown pipe character injection (commit 1c005f4)
- graph.rs flaky timestamp test (commit 27c3dbf)
- agents.rs data loss on unreadable files (commit 89416cd)
- tui.rs saturating scroll offset (prior session)

## Remaining Hardening (see subtasks)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:25.854156946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:25.854156946Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality","robustness"]}
-{"id":"bd-hpt8.1","title":"Add named constant for seconds-per-day (86400) in analysis code","description":"Multiple files use the magic number 86400 (seconds per day) or 86_400_000 (milliseconds per day) inline:

  src/analysis/alerts.rs:179 — let days = inactivity.num_seconds() as f64 / 86_400.0;
  src/analysis/causal.rs:928 — total_duration_ms / 86_400_000

While these are correct and well-understood constants, a named constant improves readability:

  const SECONDS_PER_DAY: f64 = 86_400.0;
  const MS_PER_DAY: u64 = 86_400_000;

SCOPE: Define in a shared location (analysis/mod.rs or model.rs) and replace inline uses. This is purely cosmetic — no behavior change.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":10,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:35.387225605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:35.387225605Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hpt8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hpt8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:35.387225605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-hpt8.2","title":"Document the recipe.rs has_blockers filter logic with a clarifying comment","description":"In src/analysis/recipe.rs lines 235-242, the has_blockers filter has an empty if-body that multiple code reviewers flagged as confusing:

  if recipe.filters.has_blockers == Some(true) && !actionable_ids.contains(&rec.id) {
      // has_blockers = true means only show items WITH blockers
      // (items NOT in actionable list)
  } else if recipe.filters.has_blockers == Some(true) && actionable_ids.contains(&rec.id) {
      return false;
  }

The logic is correct (keep non-actionable items when has_blockers=true, reject actionable items), but the empty if-body is a code smell. It could be simplified to:

  if recipe.filters.has_blockers == Some(true) && actionable_ids.contains(&rec.id) {
      return false;
  }

This eliminates the confusing empty branch while preserving identical behavior.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":10,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:45.406826578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:45.406826578Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hpt8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hpt8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:45.406826578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-hpt8.3","title":"Add AGENTS.md documentation for scoring weight rationale","description":"The 8-component impact score weights in triage.rs (W_PAGERANK=0.22, W_BETWEENNESS=0.20, etc.) and the urgency scores (in_progress=1.0, open=0.8, review=0.7, blocked=0.5) lack inline documentation explaining WHY these specific values were chosen.

The README has a high-level description of the scoring model (lines 160-182) but doesn't explain the specific numeric choices. Adding brief rationale comments near the const declarations would help future contributors understand the design intent without needing to read the README.

EXAMPLE:
  // PageRank gets the highest weight because global graph centrality is the
  // strongest signal for identifying structurally important work. The Go legacy
  // tool uses 0.22 — do not change without updating conformance fixtures.
  const W_PAGERANK: f64 = 0.22;","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","estimated_minutes":20,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:56.865038317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:56.865038317Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","documentation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hpt8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hpt8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:56.865038317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-hpt8","title":"Epic: Robustness improvements from deep code review","description":"During a comprehensive deep code review of all 35 source files, several robustness improvements were identified. Most bugs found were already fixed in prior commits, but some hardening opportunities remain.

- Off-by-one errors in loops and indexing
- Logic errors in conditionals
- Resource leaks
- SQL injection (export_sqlite.rs)

## Result
Only 1 bare .unwrap() exists in production code (analysis/advanced.rs:701) and it is safe by algorithmic invariant. All other unwraps are either in test code or use safe variants (unwrap_or, unwrap_or_default, unwrap_or_else).

## Previously Fixed Bugs (for context)
- plan.rs track ID numbering gaps (commit 1c005f4)
- brief.rs markdown pipe character injection (commit 1c005f4)
- graph.rs flaky timestamp test (commit 27c3dbf)
- agents.rs data loss on unreadable files (commit 89416cd)
- tui.rs saturating scroll offset (prior session)

+{"id":"bd-hpt8.2","title":"Document the recipe.rs has_blockers filter logic with a clarifying comment","description":"In src/analysis/recipe.rs lines 235-242, the has_blockers filter has an empty if-body that multiple code reviewers flagged as confusing:

  if recipe.filters.has_blockers == Some(true) && !actionable_ids.contains(&rec.id) {
      // has_blockers = true means only show items WITH blockers
      // (items NOT in actionable list)
  } else if recipe.filters.has_blockers == Some(true) && actionable_ids.contains(&rec.id) {
      return false;
  }

The logic is correct (keep non-actionable items when has_blockers=true, reject actionable items), but the empty if-body is a code smell. It could be simplified to:

  if recipe.filters.has_blockers == Some(true) && actionable_ids.contains(&rec.id) {
      return false;
  }

This eliminates the confusing empty branch while preserving identical behavior.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":10,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:45.406826578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:18.172810770Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:18.172268894Z","close_reason":"Simplified to single conditional with clarifying comment (commit 86593df)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hpt8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hpt8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:45.406826578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-hpt8.3","title":"Add AGENTS.md documentation for scoring weight rationale","description":"The 8-component impact score weights in triage.rs (W_PAGERANK=0.22, W_BETWEENNESS=0.20, etc.) and the urgency scores (in_progress=1.0, open=0.8, review=0.7, blocked=0.5) lack inline documentation explaining WHY these specific values were chosen.

The README has a high-level description of the scoring model (lines 160-182) but doesn't explain the specific numeric choices. Adding brief rationale comments near the const declarations would help future contributors understand the design intent without needing to read the README.

EXAMPLE:
  // PageRank gets the highest weight because global graph centrality is the
  // strongest signal for identifying structurally important work. The Go legacy
  // tool uses 0.22 — do not change without updating conformance fixtures.
  const W_PAGERANK: f64 = 0.22;","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","estimated_minutes":20,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:56.865038317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:18.763915104Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:18.763203927Z","close_reason":"Added rationale docs for scoring weights in triage.rs (commit 86593df)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","documentation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hpt8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hpt8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:56.865038317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hzhd","title":"Epic: Eliminate remaining flaky and environment-sensitive tests","description":"Several test issues were identified and fixed during this session:

ALREADY FIXED:
- graph.rs timestamp comparison test (stripped generated_at before comparison, commit 27c3dbf)
- TUI history snapshot git commit count (redacted via redact_git_commit_count(), commit 9f8f265)
- 7 TUI test assertions updated for banner/picker/sort changes (commit 700a18a)
- export_pages e2e PascalCase insights key (commit 108350f)

REMAINING CONCERNS:
- Test data uses hard-coded 2025-2026 dates that will eventually look 'stale' relative to staleness calculations (14/30/90 day thresholds)
- Some tests depend on the real git history of the bvr repo (e.g., history mode tests)
- INSTA_UPDATE=always is needed when rendering changes affect snapshots

The project is in good shape (1298 lib + 78 conformance + 362 integration = 1660+ tests all passing) but these latent issues could cause failures in the future.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:11.688871935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:11.688871935Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stability","testing"]}
-{"id":"bd-hzhd.1","title":"Use relative dates in test fixtures to avoid staleness-dependent failures","description":"Many test fixtures use absolute dates like ts('2025-01-15T10:00:00Z') or '2026-03-08T18:00:00Z'. These dates interact with staleness calculations (14-day warning, 30-day critical, 90-day cap) in triage.rs and alerts.rs.

As time passes, what was once a 'fresh' test issue becomes 'stale', potentially changing test results. Currently the tests pass because the staleness normalization uses relative time, but some assertion values may drift.

AFFECTED FILES (sample):
- src/model.rs lines 568-674 (8+ test fixtures with 2025 dates)
- src/analysis/triage.rs test fixtures
- src/export_sqlite.rs lines 1136-1201 (2026 dates)
- src/analysis/causal.rs test fixtures

APPROACH:
- Replace absolute timestamps with chrono::Utc::now() - Duration offsets
- Use helper functions like 'days_ago(n)' for clarity
- Ensure staleness-sensitive tests use dates relative to 'now'
- Keep conformance fixture dates absolute (they match Go reference output)

RISK: Changing dates in test fixtures may require updating many assertion values. Should be done carefully with snapshot updates.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:25.017690744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:25.017690744Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:25.017690744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-hzhd.2","title":"Isolate history mode tests from real git repo state","description":"The TUI history mode tests (snap_history_git_mode, e2e_journey_history_deep_dive) read from the actual git history of the bvr repository. This means:

1. The commit count changes with every new commit (now redacted via redact_git_commit_count)
2. Commit SHAs in snapshots reference real commits that may be rebased/squashed
3. The test depends on running from within the bvr git repo

CURRENT MITIGATION:
- redact_git_commit_count() (added commit 9f8f265) masks the volatile commit count
- Snapshots capture the current rendering without asserting specific commit SHAs

IDEAL FIX:
- Create a temporary git repo in the test with known commits
- Set BvrApp.repo_root to the temp repo
- This makes the test fully deterministic and independent of the real repo

COMPLEXITY: Medium — requires understanding how the TUI discovers and reads git history, and setting up a realistic-enough temp repo with commits that reference test issue IDs.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:39.957070215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:02.152006163Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:39.957070215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:02.151189447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
